sqlite_nan_resolution.question: Does SQLite SUM over a column containing NaN propagate NaN (nanReviewer) or collapse to NULL->0.0 (c1Reviewer)?
sqlite_nan_resolution.test: .venv/bin/python: CREATE TABLE t(cost_usd REAL); INSERT [1.0, NaN, 2.0]; SELECT COALESCE(SUM(cost_usd),0.0)
sqlite_nan_resolution.result: SUM([1.0, NaN, 2.0]) = 3.0 (finite); SUM([NaN]) = 0.0. SQLite stores NaN as NULL and SUM skips NULL.
sqlite_nan_resolution.conclusion: c1Reviewer correct; nanReviewer's 'SUM propagates NaN' WRONG. NO reporting-NaN exposure. get_session_cost / get_video_cost / get_shot_spent are all NaN-safe from persisted rows. Residual closed.

observations_non_blocking: No motion/final per-shot budget veto: _shot_over_budget is registered only in _rules_for_image (image gate). Pre-existing, by-design, UNDOCUMENTED — candidate one-line inventory note (deliberate exemption) or follow-on row.

C-1 injection mutates the shot dict in-place (shot['spent_usd']=...) rather than a shallow copy. Gate-correct (value refreshed via get_shot_spent before every pass -> no stale-read; SQLite is the source of truth, not the persisted field). At most a cosmetic field in a persisted snapshot; no action.
